# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.210.0/http/server.ts";

// Define an interface for the expected module structure
interface PluginModule {
  name: string;
  run: (config: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<string>;
  // Add other properties that your plugins might have
}

// Function to validate and normalize module paths
function isValidModulePath(path: string): boolean {
  // Basic validation: must be an HTTPS URL or a relative path (for local testing/deploy)
  // Deno Deploy typically supports HTTPS URLs for external modules.
  // Relative paths might work for modules deployed alongside the main app.
  return path.startsWith("https://") || path.startsWith("./") || path.startsWith("../");
}

// Function to dynamically load and run modules
async function loadAndRunModules(
  modulePaths: string[],
  config: Record<string, unknown>
): Promise<string[]> {
  const results: string[] = [];

  for (const path of modulePaths) {
    if (!isValidModulePath(path)) {
      results.push(`Error: Invalid module path "${path}"`);
      continue;
    }

    try {
      console.log(`Attempting to import module: ${path}`);
      const module = await import(path);

      // Check if the module conforms to the PluginModule interface
      if (typeof module.name === "string" && typeof module.run === "function") {
        const plugin = module as PluginModule;
        console.log(`Successfully loaded plugin: ${plugin.name}`);
        const result = await plugin.run(config);
        results.push(`Plugin '${plugin.name}' output: ${result}`);
      } else {
        results.push(`Error: Module at "${path}" does not conform to the PluginModule interface.`);
      }
    } catch (e) {
      console.error(`Failed to import module ${path}:`, e);
      results.push(`Error importing module "${path}": ${e.message}`);
    }
  }
  return results;
}

serve(async (req: Request) => {
  const url = new URL(req.url);

  // 1. Get module paths from environment variables
  // Example: DENO_DEPLOY_MODULES="https://example.com/pluginA.ts,./plugins/pluginB.ts"
  const envModules = Deno.env.get("DENO_DEPLOY_MODULES");
  const envModulePaths = envModules ? envModules.split(",").map(s => s.trim()).filter(Boolean) : [];

  // 2. Get module paths from query parameters
  // Example: /?modules=https://example.com/pluginC.ts,./plugins/pluginD.ts
  const queryModulesParam = url.searchParams.get("modules");
  const queryModulePaths = queryModulesParam ? queryModulesParam.split(",").map(s => s.trim()).filter(Boolean) : [];

  // Combine and deduplicate module paths
  const allModulePaths = Array.from(new Set([...envModulePaths, ...queryModulePaths]));

  if (allModulePaths.length === 0) {
    return new Response(
      "No modules specified. Please set DENO_DEPLOY_MODULES environment variable or use the 'modules' query parameter.",
      { status: 400 }
    );
  }

  // Example configuration to pass to plugins
  const pluginConfig = {
    requestUrl: req.url,
    userAgent: req.headers.get("User-Agent") || "unknown",
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    // Add other relevant configuration here
  };

  const results = await loadAndRunModules(allModulePaths, pluginConfig);

  const responseBody = `
    <h1>Deno Deploy Plugin System</h1>
    <p>Modules attempted to load: ${allModulePaths.join(", ")}</p>
    <h2>Plugin Results:</h2>
    <ul>
      ${results.map(result => `<li>${result}</li>`).join("")}
    </ul>
    <h2>Passed Configuration:</h2>
    <pre>${JSON.stringify(pluginConfig, null, 2)}</pre>
  `;

  return new Response(responseBody, {
    headers: { "Content-Type": "text/html; charset=utf-8" },
  });
});
